name: LexisNexis API
what_it_does: Enterprise REST API from LexisNexis providing programmatic access to comprehensive legal research, news, company information, and regulatory content through OAuth 2.0 authenticated endpoints.
use_cases: Enterprise legal research platforms requiring broad case law and statutory coverage
Due diligence and risk assessment tools combining legal and business intelligence data
Regulatory compliance monitoring applications tracking changes in law and regulation
News and public records enrichment for legal matter tracking and client intake
Law firm knowledge management systems integrating authoritative legal content
not_for: Small firms or individuals without enterprise LexisNexis subscriptions
Projects needing self-serve access without sales engagement
Use cases requiring Westlaw-specific features like KeyCite
best_when: You have an enterprise LexisNexis subscription and need to embed authoritative legal research, news monitoring, or business intelligence into custom applications with OAuth 2.0 integration.
avoid_when: You need low-cost or free legal data access, rapid API prototyping without a procurement process, or use cases where CourtListener covers your case law needs.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 with client credentials and authorization code flows; scopes control access to different content types (case law, news, company data); credentials tied to enterprise subscription

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise subscription required; pricing varies significantly by content packages and user count; typically comparable to or higher than Westlaw
pricing.notes: No self-serve pricing or trial access. Must contact LexisNexis sales. API pricing is separate from standard subscription and negotiated based on usage volume and content scope.

performance.rate_limits: Negotiated per subscription; enterprise tiers support high throughput; rate limit headers provided in responses

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OAuth error responses follow RFC 6749 standards; legal content errors are less consistently documented; enterprise support fills gaps
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only research and retrieval endpoints; safe to retry on transient failures
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth token management required — access tokens expire and must be refreshed
Content package scoping means agents may get 403s for content types not included in subscription
Search relevance tuning for legal queries requires domain expertise to parameterize correctly
Terms of service restrict use of retrieved content for AI training
API documentation gated behind developer portal requiring separate registration
Response schemas can vary across content types (case law vs. news vs. company data) requiring flexible parsing

security_score_components.security_notes: Strong enterprise security posture with OAuth 2.0, GDPR compliance for EU data, and SOC2 certification; appropriate for sensitive legal and business intelligence data
